core_mechanism: Estimation of a self-similar (scale-invariant) power-law scaling exponent from the two-point correlation function of a fluctuating field across a range of separations, with an explicit correction for how the coarse-grained observable is constructed distorting the correlation at small separations
brief_reason: The abstract fits a power law to the two-point correlation function of energy dissipation to extract a scaling exponent (mu=0.20), and attributes the breakdown of that scaling at small separations to how the dissipation field is operationally constructed (surrogacy).
